steps.title: Find the shared claim
steps.content: The question asks what both authors would most likely agree with. Text 1 is confident that UNESCO's City of Gastronomy designation brought awareness to Rasht's food culture and boosted tourism. Text 2 is more cautious, saying cities still need effective marketing to benefit fully from the designation.

steps.title: Balance confidence and caution
steps.content: Both texts can support a moderate claim that the designation can benefit a city's food scene. Text 1 says that benefit happened in Rasht, while Text 2 says the benefit is possible but may require intentional promotion to reach visitors.

steps.title: Avoid one-sided claims
steps.content:
• Choice A goes beyond both texts by claiming benefits well beyond increased tourism.
  • Choice B fits Text 2's caution better than Text 1's confident statement about Rasht.
  • Choice D conflicts with Text 1, which says the honor is well known among tourists who visit.
The answer is C.
